entities.name: CARBON
entities.description: Chemical element and nutrient whose fluxes are studied from headwaters to the ocean

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: Chemical element and nutrient whose fluxes are studied from headwaters to the ocean

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: Chemical element and nutrient whose fluxes are studied from headwaters to the ocean

entities.name: WATER EROSION
entities.description: Process providing major links in global cycles of carbon, nitrogen, and phosphorus

entities.name: SUBTROPICAL CLIMATE
entities.description: Climate type characterized by mean annual precipitation greater than 980 mm yr-1

entities.name: TEMPERATE CLIMATE
entities.description: Climate type found on highlands with mean annual precipitation greater than 2000 mm yr-1

entities.name: THUKELA BASIN
entities.description: Mountainous basin in South Africa used as the study area

entities.name: SOUTH AFRICA
entities.description: Country where the Thukela basin is located

entities.name: OPEN GRASSLAND
entities.description: One of the major land uses in the Thukela basin

entities.name: CROPLAND
entities.description: One of the major land uses in the Thukela basin

entities.name: BUSHLAND
entities.description: One of the major land uses in the Thukela basin

entities.name: OCEAN
entities.description: The final destination for exported carbon, nitrogen, and phosphorus fluxes

entities.name: RAINFALL PARAMETERS
entities.description: Environmental factors that correlate strongly with hillslope nutrient fluxes

entities.name: RIVER NETWORK
entities.description: The system of waterways transporting nutrients from the landscape to the ocean

entities.name: DEPOSITION
entities.description: The dominant loss process at the hillslope level

entities.name: MINERALIZATION
entities.description: Process in the river network contributing to the shift from particulate to dissolved nutrient forms

entities.name: BIOTIC UPTAKE
entities.description: Process in the river network contributing to the shift from particulate to dissolved nutrient forms

entities.name: PARTICULATE FORM
entities.description: A form of nutrient transport that dominates hillslope fluxes

entities.name: DISSOLVED FORM
entities.description: A form of nutrient transport that dominates the lower basin zone
